days.title: Yesterday I worked: the aorist and the -х ending (I)

vocab.exampleEn: I worked in the office yesterday.

grammar.title: The aorist: the first-person ending -х
grammar.explanation: The aorist is the simple past tense for completed actions, like English 'I worked, I studied'. Today we focus on the 'I' form, which always ends in -х. For most verbs you take the present stem and add -их or -ах: работя → работих (I worked), уча → учих (I studied). Note that Bulgarian still uses the present-tense 'to be' (съм, си, е...) normally; the aorist is only for action verbs. Remember there is no infinitive in Bulgarian — verbs are named by their 'I' present form.

examples.english: Yesterday I worked all day.
I studied Bulgarian in the office.
I was tired because I worked a lot.

reading.title: Моят ден вчера
reading.text: Вчера работих много. Учих български цял ден. Бях много уморен.
reading.translation: Yesterday I worked a lot. I studied Bulgarian all day. I was very tired.

days.speakingPrompt: In Bulgarian, say two things you did yesterday using the -х 'I' form (e.g. 'Вчера работих...', 'Учих...'). End by saying whether you were tired.

days.title: What did you do? The 'you' and 'he/she' forms (no ending)

vocab.exampleEn: The book is on the table.

grammar.title: The aorist: 'you' and 'he/she' have no ending
grammar.explanation: In the aorist, the 'you (singular)' form and the 'he/she/it' form are usually identical and carry NO personal ending — just the stem (often with a vowel). From работих (I worked) we get работи (you/he/she worked); from учих → учи; from четох → чете; from писах → писа. To make a yes/no question, put the particle ли right after the verb: Ти работи ли вчера? (Did you work yesterday?).

examples.english: Did you work yesterday?
He read a book all day.
She wrote a letter in the office.

reading.title: Кратък разговор
reading.text: — Ти работи ли вчера? — Не. Учих и четох книга. Тя писа писмо.
reading.translation: — Did you work yesterday? — No. I studied and read a book. She wrote a letter.

days.speakingPrompt: Ask a friend in Bulgarian whether they worked or studied yesterday using ...ли. Then report one thing that 'he' (той) and one thing that 'she' (тя) did, using the no-ending aorist forms.

days.title: We did it together: the -хме ending (we)

vocab.exampleEn: We talked for a long time.
We had dinner at a restaurant.
The restaurant is next to the office.

grammar.title: The aorist: the 'we' ending -хме
grammar.explanation: The 'we' form of the aorist is made by adding -хме to the same stem you used for 'I'. From работих → работихме (we worked); from говорих → говорихме (we talked); from гледах → гледахме (we watched). The vowel before -хме (и or а) matches the vowel in the 'I' form. Use the preposition за to mean 'about': говорихме за работата (we talked about work).

examples.english: Yesterday we watched a film together.
We talked about work for a long time.
We had dinner at the restaurant next to the office.

reading.title: Вечерта вчера
reading.text: Вчера гледахме филм заедно. После вечеряхме в ресторанта. Говорихме за работата.
reading.translation: Yesterday we watched a film together. Afterwards we had dinner at the restaurant. We talked about work.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe in Bulgarian what you and a friend did together yesterday evening. Use at least two -хме 'we' forms (e.g. гледахме, вечеряхме) and the word заедно.

days.title: And you all? The -хте ending (you plural)

vocab.exampleEn: Did you shop at the store?
In the morning I bought bread.

grammar.title: The aorist: the 'you plural' ending -хте
grammar.explanation: For 'you (plural or polite)' add -хте to the aorist stem: работихте (you worked), купихте (you bought), пазарувахте (you shopped). It mirrors the -хме 'we' ending but with -те. Combine it with какво (what) to ask about yesterday: Какво купихте вчера? (What did you buy yesterday?). Add ли for yes/no questions: Пазарувахте ли? (Did you shop?).

examples.english: What did you buy at the shop?
Did you work yesterday morning?
In the morning you shopped, then you had dinner.

reading.title: В магазина
reading.text: — Какво купихте вчера? — Купихме хляб в магазина. Пазарувахте ли и вие?
reading.translation: — What did you buy yesterday? — We bought bread at the shop. Did you shop too?

days.speakingPrompt: Imagine asking two friends in Bulgarian what they did yesterday. Ask one question with какво and -хте (e.g. Какво купихте?) and one yes/no question with ли and a -хте form.

days.title: They did it: the -ха ending (they)

vocab.exampleEn: The children played in the park.
The park is big and nice.

grammar.title: The aorist: the 'they' ending -ха
grammar.explanation: The 'they' form of the aorist adds -ха to the aorist stem: играха (they played), пътуваха (they travelled), почиваха (they rested), работиха (they worked). The vowel before -ха matches the 'I' form's vowel. Note the plural definite article is the suffix -та on this noun: деца → децата (the children), so 'the children played' is Децата играха.

examples.english: The children played football in the park.
They travelled to Sofia yesterday.
Afterwards they rested all day.

reading.title: Децата вчера
reading.text: Вчера децата играха футбол в парка. После те почиваха. Не пътуваха до София.
reading.translation: Yesterday the children played football in the park. Afterwards they rested. They did not travel to Sofia.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe in Bulgarian what 'they' (те) did yesterday using at least two -ха forms (e.g. играха, пътуваха, почиваха). Mention where, using a preposition like в or до.

days.title: The whole story: all six aorist endings together

vocab.exampleEn: We got up early in the morning.
I drank coffee in the morning.

grammar.title: Putting the whole aorist together
grammar.explanation: Now combine all six endings on one verb. Example with работя (to work): работих (I), работи (you/he/she), работихме (we), работихте (you pl.), работиха (they). The pattern: stem + -х / – / – / -хме / -хте / -ха. The single ending that changes most is the -х family (х, хме, хте, ха); 'you' and 'he/she' share the bare stem. Use time words рано/късно/сутринта/после to build a sequence of yesterday's events.

examples.english: I got up early and drank coffee.

examples.russian: Тя яде закуска, после работихме заедно.
examples.english: She ate breakfast, then we worked together.

examples.russian: Те станаха късно и почиваха цял ден.
examples.english: They got up late and rested all day.

reading.title: Цялата сутрин
reading.text: Вчера станах рано. Пих кафе и ядох закуска. После работихме заедно цял ден.
reading.translation: Yesterday I got up early. I drank coffee and ate breakfast. Afterwards we worked together all day.

days.speakingPrompt: Tell the story of your morning yesterday in Bulgarian in 3–4 sentences. Use at least three different aorist forms (different persons), and link them with рано, после, and цял ден.

days.title: Review: yesterday in the aorist

grammar.title: Recap: the aorist endings -х, –, –, -хме, -хте, -ха
grammar.explanation: This week you learned the aorist, the simple past for completed actions. Endings on the aorist stem: I = -х (работих), you/he/she = no ending (работи), we = -хме (работихме), you pl. = -хте (работихте), they = -ха (работиха). Remember: Bulgarian keeps the present-tense 'to be' (съм, си, е, сме, сте, са) and has no infinitive, so verbs are named by the 'I' form. Make questions with ли after the verb, and ask 'what' with какво. There are no noun cases — use prepositions (в, на, до, от, с, за) and word order. The definite article stays a suffix: книгата, паркът, децата.

examples.english: Yesterday I worked, then we watched a film.
What did you buy at the shop?

examples.russian: Децата играха в парка и пътуваха до София.
examples.english: The children played in the park and travelled to Sofia.

reading.title: Какво прави вчера?
reading.text: — Какво прави вчера? — Станах рано, пих кафе и работих. После гледахме филм заедно. — А децата? — Те играха в парка.
reading.translation: — What did you do yesterday? — I got up early, drank coffee and worked. Afterwards we watched a film together. — And the children? — They played in the park.

days.speakingPrompt: Have a short imaginary conversation about yesterday in Bulgarian. Answer the question 'Какво прави вчера?' with at least four aorist verbs across different persons (I, we, they). Ask your partner one ли question and one какво question.
